stats.changes:
please note that users numbers here are smart contracts that trigger oracle price updates
there could be 100x more users behind each address
stats.lessons: Still far away from the objective stated in the initial LTIPP request -- need for us to do better in activity

actuals.summary: From July on, all statements shall be done in due time
actuals.disclosures: Had delays in settling this 1st bi weekly reimbursements - trying to settle how exactly how to determine ETH <> ARB denomination for the cost incurred

planned.changes:
No changes vs grant request

All gas costs incurred to update price within the Pyth oracle SC and then using it will be reimbursed to dApps
planned.expectations: Doing backward looking but apps were scaling up this week

stats.changes: No changes applied overall -- just further push for existing dapps to better integrate Pyth & new ones to onboard
stats.lessons: Preparing for SNX deployment on Arbitrum over July

actuals.summary:
increased usage of the oracle - about 3-4x in terms of price updates triggered - hence the increased ARB disbursements on this bi weekly occurence

Couple new protocols are launching on Arbitrum so expecting number to further increase in the next couple of weeks
actuals.disclosures: Nothing new compared to the 1st biweekly payout

planned.changes:
Nothing changed from previous 2 weeks
Still paying in ARB the ETH usd gas cost incurred by the apps powered by Pyth when doing price updates
planned.expectations: Slow increase in usage of the oracle, so higher fees incurred and so payback

stats.changes:
further work with powered by pyth apps to expand product offering, and optimize offerings to users (trade size, fees)
new apps launching
stats.lessons: reaching expected usage already and will continue to strive for more

planned.changes:
no changes in the delivery / reasons of the arbitrum grant usage - keep reimbursing costs incurred by those who update the pyth prices

further bd work for more integration and existing apps increasing their product offering
planned.expectations: finally reaching the levels of usage outlined in the initial ltipp request

stats.changes: no changes apart from more BD work on our end to find new users and further better existing ones
stats.lessons:
existing ones have overall reached a plateau which will be passed coming from more onchain activity
so now focusing more on the newer dapps

actuals.summary: Pyth usage (and so amount paid back) keeps growing which is great
actuals.disclosures: As always i keep further information about these past 2 weeks here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ef2VI76252p8lTBqMGVZAXNeJxionvfL?usp=drive_link

planned.changes: no change from previous week in how ARB grants is used or distributed
planned.expectations: pay back to dapps using pyth should continually to grow as more as launching wiht pyth or existing users gain further traction

stats.changes: consumption overall stable but costs have increased
stats.lessons: spending / gas usage is still not very predictable

actuals.summary: This week with increased volatility in the markets apps had to do more updates - and gas costs increased ; meaning this is by far the biggest growth bi weekly -- about 3x

planned.expectations: this 6th period should similar to the 5th in terms of activity or higher

stats.changes: in line with the average weeks
stats.lessons: onchain activity is still very volatility/price dependant

planned.changes: none compared to all previous payback of gas fees
planned.expectations:
this is theoretically the last occurrence of the LTIPP program
however the pyth network will be asking for an extension of this program on the Arbitrum forum this week

stats.lessons: with a quiet market, apps had less need or reasons to do more price updates (less volatility) and actyually it is good that most didnt over spend for the sake of updating prices

actuals.summary: expenses have flatlined within a quieter market

planned.expectations: updates to slightly plateau from recent growth
